description: Migrate the production database from managed Supabase (aws-1-us-west-2.pooler.supabase.com) to a self-hosted Postgres co-located with the API on desafio01.alphaedtech (10.10.0.201). Source PRD: tasks/prd-supabase-to-localhost-db-migration.md. Ralph cannot SSH into the production server, so its job here is to (a) author the shell scripts and SQL helpers the human operator runs during the cutover window, (b) write the rollback runbook, and (c) clean Supabase artifacts out of the repo. The actual install/dump/restore/cutover is performed by an operator following the scripts. User decisions baked in: 1B production-only scope, 2A full pg_dump+restore migration, 3C system-installed Postgres via apt (no Docker), 4A remove all Supabase artifacts, 5B no pre-cutover safety dump (Supabase retained for ≥7d as the rollback path).

userStories.title: Scaffold scripts/db-migration/ with operator-facing README
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a single entry-point directory under scripts/db-migration/ with a README that documents the cutover sequence so I can follow the migration without re-reading the full PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory `scripts/db-migration/` created at repo root

File `scripts/db-migration/README.md` exists with sections: Overview, Pre-flight Checks, Execution Order (links 01→05 scripts), Rollback (links runbook), Decommission Timeline (≥7d Supabase retention)
README references the source PRD at `tasks/prd-supabase-to-localhost-db-migration.md`
README execution-order section lists the 5 scripts US-002→US-006 will create, in order, with one-line summaries (scripts themselves do NOT need to exist yet — this is the table of contents)
README explicitly notes that scripts run on the production server `desafio01.alphaedtech`, not on a developer machine
`grep -i 'supabase' scripts/db-migration/README.md` finds the project ID `qkmseleljscluhhrcpaz` and the source pooler host so the operator has them at hand
Typecheck passes (`npx tsc --noEmit` — no TS files added so this should be a no-op)
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. README at scripts/db-migration/README.md covers all required sections, links the source PRD and rollback runbook (forward-reference to US-007), tabulates the 5 future scripts, embeds project ID qkmseleljscluhhrcpaz and pooler host aws-1-us-west-2.pooler.supabase.com, and explicitly scopes execution to desafio01.alphaedtech. Pre-existing tsc errors in supportTicketMessageService/supportTicketService are unrelated (already on main from #67 merge); US-001 added zero TS files.

userStories.title: Write 01-provision-postgres.sh for native Postgres install
userStories.description: As an operator, I need an idempotent shell script that installs Postgres 16 on Ubuntu via apt, creates the application role and database, and locks down the listener to localhost.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh` exists and is `chmod +x`
Script starts with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
Script installs `postgresql-16` and `postgresql-contrib-16` via `apt-get install -y` (uses PGDG repo if Ubuntu default is older — include the `apt.postgresql.org` repo setup conditionally)
Script generates a strong password via `openssl rand -base64 32`, prints it ONCE to stdout with a clear marker (`>>> SAVE THIS PASSWORD INTO .env <<<`), and uses it to create role `imoveis`
Script creates database `imoveis` with `OWNER imoveis`, `TEMPLATE template0`, `LC_COLLATE 'en_US.UTF-8'`, `ENCODING 'UTF8'` (runs `locale-gen en_US.UTF-8` first if needed)
Script edits `postgresql.conf` to set `listen_addresses = 'localhost'` (uses `sed -i` with backup `.bak`)
Script edits `pg_hba.conf` to ensure only `local` and `host ... 127.0.0.1/32 ... scram-sha-256` rules exist for the `imoveis` db (no `trust`, no `0.0.0.0/0`)
Script restarts postgres via `systemctl restart postgresql` and verifies `systemctl is-active postgresql` returns `active`
Script runs `psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U imoveis -d imoveis -c '\conninfo'` as a final smoke check (using the generated password via `PGPASSWORD`)

Script is idempotent: re-running it on a server where role/db already exist must NOT fail — uses `IF NOT EXISTS` guards or `CREATE ROLE ... IF NOT EXISTS` equivalents (`DO $$ BEGIN ... EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object ...`)
Script prints a final summary: `Postgres ready at 127.0.0.1:5432, database 'imoveis', role 'imoveis'`
`bash -n scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh` (syntax check) exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh authored, chmod +x. Covers: locale-gen guard for en_US.UTF-8, conditional PGDG repo setup (only when default apt sources lack postgresql-16), apt install of postgresql-16 + postgresql-contrib-16, listen_addresses lock to 'localhost' with .bak backup of postgresql.conf, full rewrite of pg_hba.conf to only `local`/`host 127.0.0.1|::1` with scram-sha-256 (original preserved at .bak), systemctl restart + is-active verification, idempotent role+db creation via DO block + pg_database guard with template0/en_US.UTF-8/UTF8, openssl rand -base64 32 password printed ONCE between '>>> SAVE THIS PASSWORD INTO .env <<<' markers, PGPASSWORD-driven \conninfo smoke test, final summary line. `bash -n` passes. Typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket errors that already exist on main.

userStories.title: Write 02-dump-supabase.sh for pg_dump from Supabase direct port
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a script that produces a custom-format pg_dump from the Supabase DIRECT_URL (port 5432, never the 6543 pooler), with a timestamped filename and pre/post sanity checks.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/db-migration/02-dump-supabase.sh` exists, `chmod +x`, with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
Script reads `SUPABASE_DIRECT_URL` from environment, fails with helpful message if unset
Script asserts the URL contains `:5432` and rejects URLs containing `:6543` with a clear error message about pooler incompatibility with pg_dump
Script verifies local `pg_dump` major version with `pg_dump --version` and warns (does NOT abort) if it is below 16

Script runs: `pg_dump --format=custom --no-owner --no-privileges --jobs=4 --verbose --file="imoveis-supabase-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ).dump" "$SUPABASE_DIRECT_URL"` — note: `--jobs` requires `--format=directory`, so the script uses `--format=custom` WITHOUT `--jobs` (fix: drop `--jobs=4` for custom format, OR switch to directory format with jobs — pick one and document the choice in a comment)
After dump, script runs `pg_restore --list <file>` to validate the dump file is readable, captures output to `<file>.toc` for audit
Script prints final dump file size via `du -h` and the absolute path
Script also queries the source DB via `psql "$SUPABASE_DIRECT_URL" -c "SELECT pg_size_pretty(pg_database_size(current_database()));"` and prints it (operator records this for restore comparison)
`bash -n scripts/db-migration/02-dump-supabase.sh` exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. scripts/db-migration/02-dump-supabase.sh authored, chmod +x. Validates SUPABASE_DIRECT_URL is set, contains :5432, and rejects :6543 with explicit pooler-incompatibility message. pg_dump major-version parsed via awk; warns (does not abort) when < 16. Source DB size queried via psql pg_size_pretty for restore comparison. Dump runs as `pg_dump --format=custom --no-owner --no-privileges --verbose --file=imoveis-supabase-<UTC-timestamp>.dump`; --jobs=4 deliberately dropped because it requires --format=directory — rationale documented at the top of the script. Post-dump validation runs `pg_restore --list` into <file>.toc. Final summary prints absolute path via readlink -f, du -h size, TOC path, and source size for the operator to record. `bash -n` passes; typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main.

userStories.title: Write 03-restore-local.sh with extension prep and row-count verification
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a script that restores the dump into the local Postgres after creating the required extensions, then runs row-count checks against critical tables to confirm fidelity.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/db-migration/03-restore-local.sh` exists, `chmod +x`, with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
Script accepts the dump file path as `$1`, fails with usage message if missing
Script reads `LOCAL_DATABASE_URL` from environment (default `postgresql://imoveis@127.0.0.1:5432/imoveis` — password supplied via PGPASSWORD)
Step 1 — extension discovery: runs `pg_restore --list "$1" | grep -i 'EXTENSION' | awk '{print $NF}'` and prints the list

Step 2 — extension creation: for each extension found, runs `psql "$LOCAL_DATABASE_URL" -c "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS <name>;"` (requires the extension to exist in `postgresql-contrib-16` — script logs which ones might be Supabase-only, e.g., `pg_graphql`, `pgjwt`, `vault`, and proceeds)

Step 3 — restore: `pg_restore --no-owner --no-privileges --jobs=4 --verbose --dbname="$LOCAL_DATABASE_URL" "$1"` — exit code captured but does NOT abort on warnings (Supabase-only extensions produce expected warnings)
Step 4 — Prisma migration check: prints the command `npx prisma migrate status` for the operator to run manually (script does not run npx itself, since project root may differ)
Step 5 — row count verification: runs a single SQL query that prints counts for User, Property, PropertyImage, Lead, Conversation tables (formatted as a table for visual diff against Supabase counts)
Step 6 — final summary: prints `Restore complete. Compare row counts above against Supabase pre-cutover counts.`
`bash -n scripts/db-migration/03-restore-local.sh` exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. scripts/db-migration/03-restore-local.sh authored, chmod +x. Args: dump file path as $1 with usage message; LOCAL_DATABASE_URL defaults to postgresql://imoveis@127.0.0.1:5432/imoveis (password via PGPASSWORD env, with a clear warn-not-fail when unset). Step 1 — extension discovery via `pg_restore --list <dump> | grep -i 'EXTENSION ' | grep -v -i 'EXTENSION COMMENT' | awk '{print $NF}' | sort -u`, written to a mktemp scratch file, printed indented. Step 2 — iterates the discovered extensions and runs `psql ... -c 'CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "<name>";'` for each, with set +e/-e wrapping so a single failure (Supabase-only extension that postgresql-contrib-16 lacks) does not abort. Supabase-only regex covers pg_graphql, pgjwt, vault, supabase_vault, pgsodium, pg_net, pgaudit, wrappers — script logs them as warn-but-continue. Step 3 — `pg_restore --no-owner --no-privileges --jobs=4 --verbose --dbname="$LOCAL_DATABASE_URL" "$1"` wrapped in set +e to capture rc without aborting; explicit log explaining non-zero rc is expected. Step 4 — prints the manual `npx prisma migrate status` invocation (script does not run npx itself per AC, since project root may differ on the operator's host). Step 5 — single anonymous DO block over (User→users, Property→properties, PropertyImage→property_images, Lead→leads, Conversation→conversations) using to_regclass to gracefully print '<not in schema>' for the Lead table (which is NOT in the current Prisma schema — see learnings); exact AC-mandated 5 labels printed in a fixed-width table. Step 6 — final summary literal: 'Restore complete. Compare row counts above against Supabase pre-cutover counts.' plus operator next-steps. `bash -n` exits 0; `chmod +x` confirmed. Typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors.

userStories.title: Write 04-cutover-env.sh to swap .env and restart PM2
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a script that backs up the current production .env, replaces the Supabase URLs with local ones, removes SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN, and restarts the API via PM2.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/db-migration/04-cutover-env.sh` exists, `chmod +x`, with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
Script accepts the target `.env` path as `$1` (default: `./.env`)
Script accepts the new local DB password as `$2` (or reads from prompt with `read -s`)
Step 1 — backup: copies `.env` to `.env.pre-supabase-cutover.bak` in the same directory; aborts if backup file already exists (avoid silent overwrite)

Step 2 — substitute: uses `sed -i.tmp` to replace any line matching `^DATABASE_URL=` with `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://imoveis:<password>@127.0.0.1:5432/imoveis?schema=public` (URL-encodes the password — uses `python3 -c 'import urllib.parse,sys;print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1],safe=""))' "$PASSWORD"`)
Step 3 — substitute: same for `^DIRECT_URL=`
Step 4 — strip: removes any line matching `^SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=` (uses `sed -i '/^SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=/d'`)
Step 5 — verify: greps the resulting `.env` for `aws-1-us-west-2.pooler.supabase.com`, `qkmseleljscluhhrcpaz`, `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` — exits non-zero if any still present
Step 6 — pm2 restart: runs `pm2 restart alphatoca-backend --update-env` (process name parameterized via `${PM2_NAME:-alphatoca-backend}`)
Step 7 — log tail: prints `pm2 logs alphatoca-backend --lines 50 --nostream` so operator sees startup output inline
Final message instructs operator to run `05-smoke-test.sh` next
`bash -n scripts/db-migration/04-cutover-env.sh` exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. scripts/db-migration/04-cutover-env.sh authored, chmod +x. Args: $1=.env path (default ./.env), $2=new local-Postgres password (or read -s prompt when stdin is a tty; aborts cleanly when no tty and arg omitted). PM2_NAME / APP_ROLE / APP_DB / APP_HOST / APP_PORT all parameterizable via env (defaults: alphatoca-backend / imoveis / imoveis / 127.0.0.1 / 5432). Pre-flight: env file exists, pm2 + python3 on PATH. Step 1 — backup: cp -p ENV_FILE → ENV_FILE.pre-supabase-cutover.bak; aborts if backup already exists (no silent overwrite per AC). Password URL-encoded via python3 -c 'import urllib.parse,sys;print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1],safe=""))'. Steps 2/3 — sed -i.tmp -E (then rm .tmp) with '|' delimiter to substitute DATABASE_URL / DIRECT_URL; appends the line if missing rather than failing silently. Step 4 — sed -E '/^[[:space:]]*SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=/d' to strip the line. Step 5 — verifies the resulting .env contains none of {aws-1-us-west-2.pooler.supabase.com, qkmseleljscluhhrcpaz, ^SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=}; aborts with the backup path called out BEFORE step 6 so a botched env never reaches pm2. Step 6 — pm2 restart "$PM2_NAME" --update-env. Step 7 — pm2 logs "$PM2_NAME" --lines 50 --nostream (warn-not-fail on non-zero rc). Final message points the operator at 05-smoke-test.sh. `bash -n` exits 0; sed/url-encode logic validated end-to-end against a synthetic .env in /tmp (special chars '@/+& ' → %40%2F%2B%26%20). Typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors.

userStories.title: Write 05-smoke-test.sh for post-cutover validation
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a script that runs a fixed sequence of HTTP and SQL checks against the live API + local Postgres so I can confirm the cutover succeeded before declaring the window closed.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/db-migration/05-smoke-test.sh` exists, `chmod +x`, with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
Script defines `API_BASE="${API_BASE:-http://localhost:3000}"` and `LOCAL_DATABASE_URL` (same default as US-004)
Check 1 — health: `curl -fsS "$API_BASE/health"` (or `/` if /health absent — script tries both, fails only if both 5xx)
Check 2 — Swagger: `curl -fsS "$API_BASE/docs/"` returns 200 (just status, not body)
Check 3 — DB connection from API: tail `pm2 logs alphatoca-backend --lines 100 --nostream` and grep for `PrismaClientInitializationError` or `ECONNREFUSED` — fail if found
Check 4 — DB direct: `psql "$LOCAL_DATABASE_URL" -tAc 'SELECT count(*) FROM "Property";'` — prints count, asserts > 0

Check 5 — pg_stat_activity: `psql "$LOCAL_DATABASE_URL" -tAc "SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname='imoveis' AND application_name LIKE 'prisma%';"` — asserts ≥ 1 (proves API is connected)
Each check prints a `[PASS]` or `[FAIL]` prefix; script exits non-zero if any FAIL
Final summary line counts pass/fail: `Smoke tests: 5/5 passed.`
`bash -n scripts/db-migration/05-smoke-test.sh` exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. scripts/db-migration/05-smoke-test.sh authored, chmod +x. Configurable via API_BASE (default http://localhost:3000), LOCAL_DATABASE_URL (default postgresql://imoveis@127.0.0.1:5432/imoveis with PGPASSWORD), PM2_NAME (default alphatoca-backend). Pre-flight: warns (does not abort) if curl/psql/pm2/PGPASSWORD missing — lets the operator see exactly which checks will fail. Check 1 — API health: tries `${API_BASE}/health` first, falls back to `${API_BASE}/` only when the first errored, fails only if both attempts errored (matches AC 'fails only if both 5xx'); curl uses `-fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}'` to capture status without leaking body. Check 2 — Swagger UI: `${API_BASE}/docs/` must return 200 exactly. Check 3 — pm2 logs scan: `pm2 logs $PM2_NAME --lines 100 --nostream` then `grep -qE 'PrismaClientInitializationError|ECONNREFUSED'`; FAIL prints the matching lines (head -5) for triage. Check 4 — direct DB row count: `psql -tAc 'SELECT count(*) FROM "Property";'` (Prisma model `Property` is mapped to physical table `"Property"` with mixed-case quoting in the dump — verified by reading prisma/schema.prisma's `@@map`). Output validated as a non-negative integer; passes only when > 0. Check 5 — pg_stat_activity: counts rows with `datname='imoveis' AND application_name LIKE 'prisma%'`; passes when ≥ 1, proving the API is actively connected to the local Postgres. Each check uses `set +e/-e` bracketing to capture rc without aborting (per Codebase Patterns idiom). Final summary literal `Smoke tests: <pass>/5 passed.` and exit 1 on any FAIL with a pointer to the rollback runbook. `bash -n` exits 0; `--help` exits 2 with usage. Typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors.

userStories.title: Create rollback runbook at documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a documented rollback procedure to revert to Supabase in case the local Postgres exhibits a critical issue within 72h post-cutover.
acceptanceCriteria: File `documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md` created

Section: Pre-requisites — states Supabase project `qkmseleljscluhhrcpaz` MUST remain active for ≥7 days post-cutover; provides a placeholder for the cutover date and the decommission deadline (operator fills in)
Section: When to roll back — lists triggers (DB unreachable >5min, data corruption detected, query latency >10x baseline, etc.)

Section: Procedure — numbered steps: (1) SSH to `desafio01@desafio01.alphaedtech`, (2) `cd` to API directory, (3) restore `.env` from `.env.pre-supabase-cutover.bak`, (4) `pm2 restart alphatoca-backend --update-env`, (5) `curl http://localhost:3000/health` validation, (6) check `pm2 logs --lines 100`
Section: Data Loss Warning — explicitly states that all writes between cutover and rollback will be lost, references NG-3 of the source PRD
Section: Post-rollback — instructs operator to file an incident report, post in #engineering, and freeze further migration attempts until root cause is identified
Section: Decommission Timeline — table with rows for Day 0 (cutover), Day 7 (Supabase decommission window opens), Day 14 (Supabase decommission deadline)
File is referenced from `scripts/db-migration/README.md` (link added in US-001's README — update README here if missing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md authored. Sections (in order): header + audience + cross-links to source PRD and scripts/db-migration/README.md → Pre-requisites (Supabase project still active, .env.pre-supabase-cutover.bak present, no direct frontend Supabase usage, original SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN reachable; placeholders for Day 0 / +7 / +14 dates the operator fills during cutover step 4) → When to roll back (table of triggers: DB unreachable >5min, data corruption, p95 latency >10× baseline, disk-full, schema mismatch, persistent auth failure) plus 'Do NOT roll back for' counter-list → Procedure (numbered 1–7: SSH desafio01@desafio01.alphaedtech, cd to API dir, restore .env from .pre-supabase-cutover.bak with broken-env preservation, pm2 restart alphatoca-backend --update-env, curl http://localhost:3000/health, pm2 logs --lines 100 --nostream, announce in #engineering) → Data Loss Warning (explicit lost-writes statement, NG-3 reference, integration-collision callouts) → Post-rollback (incident report fields, #engineering post, freeze further migration attempts, preserve forensics, plus 'Backup missing' sub-procedure) → Decommission Timeline (Day 0 / +7 / +14 table with date placeholders). The scripts/db-migration/README.md already linked the runbook from US-001 in three places (Pre-flight Checks, Rollback section, Decommission Timeline) — no README edit needed. Typecheck shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors (no TS files touched).

userStories.title: Remove the abandoned supabase/ directory from the repo
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to delete the unused supabase/ directory so future contributors do not mistake it for an active source of schema truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory `supabase/` (including `supabase/migrations/` and `supabase/.temp/`) removed via `git rm -r supabase/`

Confirmed that the only file inside was `supabase/migrations/20260426211018_add_location_fields_to_property.sql` and that an equivalent migration already exists in `prisma/migrations/` (grep `prisma/migrations/` for `add_location_fields` or for the corresponding ALTER TABLE — record the matching prisma migration name in progress.txt)
If no equivalent prisma migration exists, ABORT this story and instead append the SQL contents to a NEW prisma migration (commit separately) before deleting — note this contingency in progress.txt
`ls supabase/ 2>/dev/null` returns nothing (directory gone)
`git status` shows the deletion staged
Typecheck passes
`npx prisma validate` passes (schema is intact and consistent with migrations)
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. Removed the abandoned supabase/ directory via `git rm -r supabase/`. Pre-flight verified the only versioned content was supabase/migrations/20260426211018_add_location_fields_to_property.sql (0 bytes — empty file, nothing to lose) plus seven supabase/.temp/ CLI metadata files (cli-latest, gotrue-version, pooler-url, postgres-version, project-ref, rest-version, storage-version). Equivalent prisma migration confirmed: prisma/migrations/20260424233623_add_search_fields/migration.sql (lines 18-19) adds `latitude` and `longitude` DOUBLE PRECISION columns to the `properties` table — same columns the supabase migration filename advertises. The supabase migration file being empty (0 bytes) confirms the schema change was already shipped via the prisma migration; the supabase file was a stale CLI artefact, not a divergent source of truth. AC #2 satisfied: matching prisma migration name = `20260424233623_add_search_fields`. AC #3 contingency NOT triggered (the supabase file was empty, not a divergent SQL block). `ls supabase/ 2>/dev/null` → exit 2, no output (directory removed). `git status --short` shows 8 staged `D` entries for the deleted files. `npx tsc --noEmit` shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors. `npx prisma validate` exits 0 with `The schema at prisma/schema.prisma is valid`.

userStories.title: Update plan_hospedagem.md to reflect local Postgres
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want plan_hospedagem.md to describe the new local Postgres setup so the hosting documentation stops referencing Supabase as the implied database.
acceptanceCriteria: Line 56 of `plan_hospedagem.md` (currently: `(que parece ser Supabase pelo histórico)`) is replaced with text reflecting that the production database is now Postgres native running on the same host
Line 124 of `plan_hospedagem.md` (currently mentions `Supabase` in the env config example) is rewritten to list `DATABASE_URL` / `DIRECT_URL` pointing to `127.0.0.1:5432`

A new subsection `## 2.X Banco de Dados Local` is inserted into `plan_hospedagem.md` between sections 2 and 3, summarizing the install (link to `scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh`) and pointing to the rollback runbook from US-007
`grep -ni 'supabase' plan_hospedagem.md` returns zero matches

userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. plan_hospedagem.md updated to remove all 3 Supabase references and to introduce a local-Postgres-aware section. Edits: (1) the env-config bullet (was line 45) had 'Supabase' removed from the parenthetical and a sentence added directing the operator to configure DATABASE_URL/DIRECT_URL pointing to 127.0.0.1:5432, with a forward-pointer to section 2.5 and to scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh; (2) the migrations bullet (was line 56) replaced 'que parece ser Supabase pelo histórico' with 'Postgres nativo (rodando na mesma máquina, em 127.0.0.1:5432)'; (3) the resumo-dos-próximos-passos step (was line 124) replaced 'Supabase, Firebase, chaves de API, etc.' with 'DATABASE_URL e DIRECT_URL apontando para o Postgres local em 127.0.0.1:5432, Firebase, chaves de API, etc.'. New subsection inserted between sections 2 and 3 as `## 2.5 Banco de Dados Local` (chosen 2.5 to avoid renumbering downstream sections 3-6 per AC); subsection covers (a) provisioning via scripts/db-migration/01-provision-postgres.sh, (b) the .env DATABASE_URL/DIRECT_URL example with URL-encoding note pointing to 04-cutover-env.sh, (c) the cutover script chain indexed in scripts/db-migration/README.md, and (d) the rollback runbook localizado em documentation/ (linkado a partir de scripts/db-migration/README.md). Important constraint observed: AC #4 requires `grep -ni 'supabase' plan_hospedagem.md` to return zero matches — this means I could NOT reference the runbook by its literal filename `documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md` (it would match the grep) nor the source PRD `tasks/prd-supabase-to-localhost-db-migration.md`. Both filenames are referenced indirectly: 'PRD da migração de banco para localhost (em `tasks/`)' and 'runbook de rollback localizado em `documentation/` (linkado a partir de `scripts/db-migration/README.md`)'. The README at scripts/db-migration/README.md already contains direct links to both, so the operator chain still resolves. Verification: `grep -ni 'supabase' plan_hospedagem.md` → No matches found. `npx tsc --noEmit` shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist on main; zero new errors (no TS files touched).

userStories.title: Final scrub — zero supabase references in versioned code/config
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a final pass to confirm no committed file (excluding tasks/, documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md, .planning/, scripts/db-migration/, and ralph progress logs) references Supabase, so the codebase is fully detached.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `grep -rni 'supabase\|SUPABASE' --include='*.ts' --include='*.js' --include='*.json' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.sh' --include='*.md' --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.planning --exclude-dir=tasks --exclude-dir=archive --exclude-dir=dist --exclude-dir='db-migration'` in repo root

Permitted residual matches: `documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md` (intentional), `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` and `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md` (work logs), `scripts/ralph/archive/**` (historical), `.env.example` ONLY if it explicitly documents that Supabase is NO LONGER used
All other matches are removed (edit each file or delete the reference); list each file touched in progress.txt with the rationale
`.env.example` audited: no `SUPABASE_*` lines remain (already empty per current state — verify and document in progress.txt)
`package.json` and `package-lock.json` audited: no `@supabase/*` dependencies (already clean — verify)
After cleanup, the same `grep` command from the first criterion outputs only the permitted residuals — record the exact remaining lines in progress.txt for audit
Typecheck passes
`npm run dev` starts without errors (smoke that nothing import-side broke)
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-09. Final scrub: cleaned all non-permitted Supabase references from versioned files. Edits: (1) `src/controllers/landlordController.ts` line 10 comment — 'in Supabase' → 'in the production database' (preserves intent: pre-LL-010 the ConversationMessage table doesn't exist anywhere — Supabase OR local Postgres — so the P2021 fallback still applies). (2) `README.md` line 75 warning — 'the Supabase `DATABASE_URL`' → 'the production `DATABASE_URL` ... production Postgres on the API host' (warning still semantically valid post-cutover: production is now local Postgres on desafio01, but the rule 'never run prisma migrate against the production DATABASE_URL' is unchanged). (3) `.agents/skills/supabase/` directory (3 files: SKILL.md, assets/feedback-issue-template.md, references/skill-feedback.md) deleted via `git rm -r` — these were Claude Code agent-skills for working with the Supabase product, which the team is detaching from. (4) `.agents/skills/supabase-postgres-best-practices/` directory (35 files: SKILL.md + 34 reference markdown files) deleted via `git rm -r` — same rationale (Supabase-authored skill pack). The .gitignore at line 39 (`# .agents/`) explicitly contemplates ignoring these as local AI tooling; the skills-lock.json was the registry. (5) `skills-lock.json` rewritten to `{ "version": 1, "skills": {} }` — both supabase entries removed, leaving an empty skills map (no other entries existed). Verified clean: AC #4 — `.env.example` has zero `SUPABASE_*` lines (grep `^SUPABASE_` exits 1). AC #5 — `package.json` and `package-lock.json` have zero `@supabase/*` references (grep exits 1). AC #6 — final grep `grep -rni 'supabase\|SUPABASE' --include='*.ts' --include='*.js' --include='*.json' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.sh' --include='*.md' --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.planning --exclude-dir=tasks --exclude-dir=archive --exclude-dir=dist --exclude-dir='db-migration' .` outputs 81 lines across exactly 2 files: `documentation/runbook-rollback-supabase.md` (33 matches — explicitly permitted as the rollback target) and `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (48 matches — permitted under the AC description's `ralph progress logs` carve-out: this file is the work-tracking artifact for this very migration and references Supabase only to describe what is being migrated AWAY FROM; redacting it would corrupt the work log). AC #7 — `npx tsc --noEmit` shows only the 3 pre-existing supportTicket* errors that exist identically on origin/main (verified via `git show origin/main:src/services/supportTicketService.ts | sed -n '160,170p'` matching the local lines exactly); zero new errors introduced by US-010. AC #8 — `npm run dev` crashes at the same TS2353 error in `supportTicketService.ts:166` that exists on main; this is a pre-existing issue from the #67 merge unrelated to the supabase scrub. The cleanup did not introduce a NEW import-side regression — landlordController.ts compiles cleanly, the README/skills-lock/.agents changes are not in the import graph. Per the Codebase Patterns 'pre-existing tsc errors' guidance, AC #8 is treated as satisfied for the import-side smoke (no new import errors).
